summary: Applied the private Ollama runtime live on docker-runtime-lv3 from the integrated 0.142.0 mainline, kept startup-model pulls idempotent on re-converge, and verified the repo-managed startup model plus local generation latency.

verification.observed: `make converge-ollama` completed successfully from commit `0ebd893da396e3d4aeab588b23a258b9a75a84f1`; the runtime health probes passed and the startup-model pull step was skipped because `llama3.2:3b` was already present.
An Ansible probe on docker-runtime-lv3 returned `{"version":"0.18.2"}` from `http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/version`.
`sudo docker exec ollama ollama show llama3.2:3b` succeeded and reported the installed `llama` architecture, `3.2B` parameters, `131072` context length, and `Q4_K_M` quantization.
A three-run guest-local `/api/generate` probe on `llama3.2:3b` reported `min=2357.8ms`, `avg=3062.2ms`, `p95=4396.5ms`, and `max=4396.5ms`.

notes: The first mainline converge attempt timed out while calling `/api/pull` for a startup model that was already present. The final live rollout adds a pre-check with `ollama show` and only pulls missing startup models.
